  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Ticketing and Tour Officer",
  "description": "Hec Recruit is a full-service HR consulting firm with a unique approach. Since 2020 we have been helping our clients find a job best suited for their skills and interests, and helping local companies locate the best employees.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Ticketing and Tour Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Industry: Travel and Tour
Work mode: Onsite

Job Description


	Giving tickets and documents to consumers
	Monitor bookings, revenue, expenses and profitability
	Build and maintain relationships with airlines, hotoes, tour operations and vendors.


Requirements


	2+ years in ticketing and tour
	Strong knowledge of travel regulations
	Good communication skills.


Salary
N200,000 net / month (Negotiable based on experience).

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;hecrecruitment.hrconsulting@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Ticketing and Tour Officer Job at Hec Recruit




        
    

        
        Hec Recruit is a full-service HR consulting firm with a unique approach. Since 2020 we have been helping our clients find a job best suited for their skills and interests, and helping local companies locate the best employees.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Ticketing and Tour Officer

Job Location: Lagos Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Industry: Travel and Tour
Work mode: Onsite

Job Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Giving tickets and documents to consumers
	Monitor bookings, revenue, expenses and profitability
	Build and maintain relationships with airlines, hotoes, tour operations and vendors.


Requirements


	2+ years in ticketing and tour
	Strong knowledge of travel regulations
	Good communication skills.


Salary
N200,000 net / month (Negotiable based on experience).

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Administrative Officer",
  "description": "People Capacity Management is a human resources and management consulting firm located in Lagos, Nigeria. We provide services, expertise and resources to both the private and public sectors through our deep knowledge of issues specific to industries and sectors.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Administrative Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities


	Manage calendars, schedule meetings, and prepare meeting materials for senior staff.
	Handle inbound and outbound communications, including emails, phone calls, and visitor reception.
	Process travel arrangements, accommodation, and expense reports.
	Maintain accurate records, both physical and digital, and oversee the filing system.
	Coordinate office procurement, inventory control, and vendor payments.
	Support HR with onboarding, probation tracking, and other admin?related duties&nbsp;and other admin?related duties


Qualifications


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Business Administration, Public Administration, or related field.
	3?4?years of proven administrative experience (previous admin officer or similar role).
	Male gender preferred
	Excellent command of Microsoft Office Suite and familiarity with office equipment (copiers, scanners, etc.).
	Strong organizational skills, attention to detail, and ability to prioritize in a multitasking environment.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;recruitment@peoplecapacitymanagement.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Administrative Officer Job at People Capacity Management




        
    

        
        People Capacity Management is a human resources and management consulting firm located in Lagos, Nigeria. We provide services, expertise and resources to both the private and public sectors through our deep knowledge of issues specific to industries and sectors.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Administrative Officer

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Manage calendars, schedule meetings, and prepare meeting materials for senior staff.
	Handle inbound and outbound communications, including emails, phone calls, and visitor reception.
	Process travel arrangements, accommodation, and expense reports.
	Maintain accurate records, both physical and digital, and oversee the filing system.
	Coordinate office procurement, inventory control, and vendor payments.
	Support HR with onboarding, probation tracking, and other admin?related duties and other admin?related duties


Qualifications


	Bachelor’s Degree in Business Administration, Public Administration, or related field.
	3?4?years of proven administrative experience (previous admin officer or similar role).
	Male gender preferred
	Excellent command of Microsoft Office Suite and familiarity with office equipment (copiers, scanners, etc.).
	Strong organizational skills, attention to detail, and ability to prioritize in a multitasking environment.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Finance Manager (Chartered Accountant)",
  "description": "Emel Hospital&nbsp;Festac Town, Lagos, Nigeria is a multi-specialists&rsquo; hospital, situated at 21 Road, Z Close in Festac Town, Amuwo Odofin Local Government Lagos, Nigeria. Emel Hospital is a purpose-built 44 bed hospital situated on a piece of land specially approved for its purpose by the Federal Housing Authority.

Emel Hospital was established in 1988 by Dr. Olufemi Mobolaji-Lawal, a consultant paediatrician. Dr. Mobolaji-Lawal trained as a doctor at the University College Hospital, Ibadan and as a paediatrician in the United Kingdom. Prior to setting Emel Hospital, he worked for some time as consultant paediatrician at Ikeja General Hospital (now Lagos State University Teaching Hospital), Lagos. He is the board chairman and his wife, Dr. (Mrs) Margaret Mobolaji-Lawal is a non-executive director.

The hospital was declared open on the 9th of December 1988 by the then Honorable Minister of Health of Nigeria, Prof. Olikoye Ransome-Kuti. It is the first private hospital in Lagos, Nigeria to be designated &ldquo;Baby Friendly Hospital&rdquo; by the Federal Ministry of Health in 1993. We are a well-recognized and foremost pediatric referral centre in Amuwo Odofin area and its environs, as well as providing general medical care and specialist services in obstetrics and gynaecology, internal medicine and various surgical specialities.

Our facility is fully computerized and staffed with professionals to deliver excellent primary medical and specialist&#39;s services. We actively promote excellence with a view to ensuring complete client satisfaction at all times.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Finance Manager (Chartered Accountant)

Job Location:&nbsp;Festac, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Reports To: Director of Strategy &amp; Innovation

Job Summary


	The Finance Manager will oversee the financial operations of the hospital, ensuring efficient financial management, regulatory compliance, and sustainability.
	The role requires a Chartered Accountant with strong corporate and healthcare finance experience, responsible for managing budgeting, revenue cycles (including NHIS, HMOs, and private billing), financial reporting, and strategic financial planning.


Key Responsibilities


	Prepare and present monthly, quarterly, and annual financial reports to management
	Develop and manage the hospital&rsquo;s budgets, forecasts, and financial plans
	Oversee revenue cycle management, including NHIS, HMOs, retainerships, and private patient billing
	Monitor income streams and expenditure, ensuring optimal cost control and revenue optimization
	Ensure compliance with healthcare regulations, tax laws, and financial standards
	Manage cash flow, banking relationships, and financial risk
	Supervise billing, collections, and reconciliation processes across departments
	Coordinate internal and external audits, ensuring transparency and accountability
	Implement and enforce financial policies, controls, and standard operating procedures (SOPs)
	Provide financial insights and advisory support for strategic decision-making and service expansion
	Collaborate with clinical and administrative teams to improve financial efficiency and service delivery
	Oversee procurement financial processes, including vendor payments and cost evaluations


Qualifications &amp; Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Accounting, Finance, or related discipline
	Chartered Accountant (ACA, ACCA)&ndash; mandatory
	7&ndash;10 years of experience, preferably in a hospital or healthcare environment, with at least 3-5years in a managerial role
	Strong knowledge of hospital billing systems, NHIS, HMO processes, and healthcare financial operations
	Proficiency in accounting software, hospital management systems, and Microsoft Excel
	Sound understanding of financial regulations and compliance in healthcare
	Strong analytical, leadership, and problem-solving skills


Key Competencies:


	Healthcare Financial Management
	Revenue Cycle Management (NHIS/HMOs)
	Financial Reporting &amp; Analysis
	Cost Control &amp; Budgeting
	Leadership &amp; Team Management
	Compliance &amp; Risk Management
	Strategic Thinking &amp; Decision Support.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;b.fadipe@emelhospital.org&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Finance Manager (Chartered Accountant) Job at Emel Hospital Festac Town




        
    

        
        
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    

Emel Hospital Festac Town, Lagos, Nigeria is a multi-specialists’ hospital, situated at 21 Road, Z Close in Festac Town, Amuwo Odofin Local Government Lagos, Nigeria. Emel Hospital is a purpose-built 44 bed hospital situated on a piece of land specially approved for its purpose by the Federal Housing Authority.

Emel Hospital was established in 1988 by Dr. Olufemi Mobolaji-Lawal, a consultant paediatrician. Dr. Mobolaji-Lawal trained as a doctor at the University College Hospital, Ibadan and as a paediatrician in the United Kingdom. Prior to setting Emel Hospital, he worked for some time as consultant paediatrician at Ikeja General Hospital (now Lagos State University Teaching Hospital), Lagos. He is the board chairman and his wife, Dr. (Mrs) Margaret Mobolaji-Lawal is a non-executive director.

The hospital was declared open on the 9th of December 1988 by the then Honorable Minister of Health of Nigeria, Prof. Olikoye Ransome-Kuti. It is the first private hospital in Lagos, Nigeria to be designated “Baby Friendly Hospital” by the Federal Ministry of Health in 1993. We are a well-recognized and foremost pediatric referral centre in Amuwo Odofin area and its environs, as well as providing general medical care and specialist services in obstetrics and gynaecology, internal medicine and various surgical specialities.

Our facility is fully computerized and staffed with professionals to deliver excellent primary medical and specialist's services. We actively promote excellence with a view to ensuring complete client satisfaction at all times.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Finance Manager (Chartered Accountant)

Job Location: Festac, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Reports To: Director of Strategy & Innovation

Job Summary


	The Finance Manager will oversee the financial operations of the hospital, ensuring efficient financial management, regulatory compliance, and sustainability.
	The role requires a Chartered Accountant with strong corporate and healthcare finance experience, responsible for managing budgeting, revenue cycles (including NHIS, HMOs, and private billing), financial reporting, and strategic financial planning.


Key Responsibilities


	Prepare and present monthly, quarterly, and annual financial reports to management
	Develop and manage the hospital’s budgets, forecasts, and financial plans
	Oversee revenue cycle management, including NHIS, HMOs, retainerships, and private patient billing
	Monitor income streams and expenditure, ensuring optimal cost control and revenue optimization
	Ensure compliance with healthcare regulations, tax laws, and financial standards
	Manage cash flow, banking relationships, and financial risk
	Supervise billing, collections, and reconciliation processes across departments
	Coordinate internal and external audits, ensuring transparency and accountability
	Implement and enforce financial policies, controls, and standard operating procedures (SOPs)
	Provide financial insights and advisory support for strategic decision-making and service expansion
	Collaborate with clinical and administrative teams to improve financial efficiency and service delivery
	Oversee procurement financial processes, including vendor payments and cost evaluations


Qualifications & Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting, Finance, or related discipline
	Chartered Accountant (ACA, ACCA)– mandatory
	7–10 years of experience, preferably in a hospital or healthcare environment, with at least 3-5years in a managerial role
	Strong knowledge of hospital billing systems, NHIS, HMO processes, and healthcare financial operations
	Proficiency in accounting software, hospital management systems, and Microsoft Excel
	Sound understanding of financial regulations and compliance in healthcare
	Strong analytical, leadership, and problem-solving skills


Key Competencies:


	Healthcare Financial Management
	Revenue Cycle Management (NHIS/HMOs)
	Financial Reporting & Analysis
	Cost Control & Budgeting
	Leadership & Team Management
	Compliance & Risk Management
	Strategic Thinking & Decision Support.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Content Creation, Strategy & Performance Associate",
  "description": "Mills Talents Solution is a dynamic human resources and talent acquisition firm committed to connecting top-tier professionals with leading organizations. We specialize in recruitment, workforce planning, and HR consulting services that help businesses grow and thrive. With a focus on integrity, efficiency, and results, Mills HR &amp; Talent Solution delivers quality talent solutions tailored to meet the unique needs of both employers and job seekers.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Content Creation, Strategy &amp; Performance Associate&nbsp;

Job Location:&nbsp;Yaba, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Description


	We are seeking a creative and results-driven Content Strategy &amp; Growth Specialist to lead the development and execution of high-performing content for a real estate brand.
	This role is for someone who knows how to turn content into engagement, audience growth, revenue and generate qualified leads.
	The role requires a strong understanding of platform algorithms (Instagram, LinkedIn, TikTok, and Twitter), the ability to create compelling content with effective hooks, and a proven track record of managing and scaling social media pages.
	Candidates must be able to demonstrate measurable growth and performance from platforms they have previously handled.


Key Responsibilities


	Own and drive end-to-end content strategy across Instagram, LinkedIn, TikTok, and Twitter.
	Create and execute high-performing content with strong hooks and storytelling.
	Grow social media pages organically while driving qualified leads.
	Continuously analyse performance and optimize content for better results.
	Leverage deep understanding of platform algorithms to maximize reach and visibility.


Requirements


	Proven track record of growing social media pages (portfolio required)
	Strong understanding of content performance, engagement metrics, and algorithms
	Hands-on ability to create content (video creation/editing is a plus)
	Experience in real estate is an advantage, but not mandatory
	Highly creative, analytical, and results-driven.


Salary
N250,000 - N300,000 / Month.

Note:&nbsp;Only candidates with visible growth and engagement on pages they have managed will be considered",

Content Creation, Strategy & Performance Associate Job at Mills Talents Solution




        
    

        
        Mills Talents Solution is a dynamic human resources and talent acquisition firm committed to connecting top-tier professionals with leading organizations. We specialize in recruitment, workforce planning, and HR consulting services that help businesses grow and thrive. With a focus on integrity, efficiency, and results, Mills HR & Talent Solution delivers quality talent solutions tailored to meet the unique needs of both employers and job seekers.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Content Creation, Strategy & Performance Associate 

Job Location: Yaba, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a creative and results-driven Content Strategy & Growth Specialist to lead the development and execution of high-performing content for a real estate brand.
	This role is for someone who knows how to turn content into engagement, audience growth, revenue and generate qualified leads.
	The role requires a strong understanding of platform algorithms (Instagram, LinkedIn, TikTok, and Twitter), the ability to create compelling content with effective hooks, and a proven track record of managing and scaling social media pages.
	Candidates must be able to demonstrate measurable growth and performance from platforms they have previously handled.


Key Responsibilities


	Own and drive end-to-end content strategy across Instagram, LinkedIn, TikTok, and Twitter.
	Create and execute high-performing content with strong hooks and storytelling.
	Grow social media pages organically while driving qualified leads.
	Continuously analyse performance and optimize content for better results.
	Leverage deep understanding of platform algorithms to maximize reach and visibility.


Requirements


	Proven track record of growing social media pages (portfolio required)
	Strong understanding of content performance, engagement metrics, and algorithms
	Hands-on ability to create content (video creation/editing is a plus)
	Experience in real estate is an advantage, but not mandatory
	Highly creative, analytical, and results-driven.


Salary
N250,000 - N300,000 / Month.

Note: Only candidates with visible growth and engagement on pages they have managed will be considered

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Restaurant Supervisor",
  "description": "High Caliber Nigeria Limited is a human resources/management consultancy company. We specialize in the following areas: Outsourcing, Recruitment and Placement, Human Capacity Development (Training), Staff Audit, Advisory and Human Resources Management.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Restaurant Supervisor

Job Location:&nbsp;Ikeja (GRA), Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Floor Supervisor is responsible for overseeing daily operations on the restaurant floor, ensuring excellent customer service, smooth coordination between staff, and adherence to company standards.
	The role involves supervising front-of-house staff, resolving customer concerns, and maintaining a high-quality dining experience.


Key Responsibilities


	Supervise and coordinate the activities of waiters, hostesses, and other front-of-house staff
	Ensure all guests are attended to promptly and professionally
	Monitor service standards and ensure compliance with company policies
	Handle customer complaints and resolve issues efficiently
	Support staff during peak hours to ensure smooth service flow
	Conduct pre-shift briefings and communicate daily updates to staff
	Monitor staff attendance, punctuality, grooming, and overall conduct
	Ensure proper table setup, cleanliness, and ambiance of the dining area
	Liaise with kitchen and bar teams to ensure timely order delivery
	Assist in training and onboarding of new staff
	Monitor inventory of service items (cutlery, napkins, etc.) and report shortages
	Ensure compliance with health, safety, and hygiene regulations


Requirements


	Minimum of OND / HND or equivalent qualification
	2&ndash;4 years of experience in a restaurant or hospitality environment
	Prior supervisory experience is an advantage
	Strong leadership and team management skills
	Excellent communication and problem-solving abilities
	Ability to work under pressure and handle difficult situations
	Good organizational and multitasking skills
	Flexibility to work shifts, weekends, and public holidays


Skills &amp; Competencies:

Restaurant Supervisor Job at High Caliber Nigeria Limited




        
    

        
        High Caliber Nigeria Limited is a human resources/management consultancy company. We specialize in the following areas: Outsourcing, Recruitment and Placement, Human Capacity Development (Training), Staff Audit, Advisory and Human Resources Management.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Restaurant Supervisor

Job Location: Ikeja (GRA), Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Floor Supervisor is responsible for overseeing daily operations on the restaurant floor, ensuring excellent customer service, smooth coordination between staff, and adherence to company standards.
	The role involves supervising front-of-house staff, resolving customer concerns, and maintaining a high-quality dining experience.


Key Responsibilities


	Supervise and coordinate the activities of waiters, hostesses, and other front-of-house staff
	Ensure all guests are attended to promptly and professionally
	Monitor service standards and ensure compliance with company policies
	Handle customer complaints and resolve issues efficiently
	Support staff during peak hours to ensure smooth service flow
	Conduct pre-shift briefings and communicate daily updates to staff
	Monitor staff attendance, punctuality, grooming, and overall conduct
	Ensure proper table setup, cleanliness, and ambiance of the dining area
	Liaise with kitchen and bar teams to ensure timely order delivery
	Assist in training and onboarding of new staff
	Monitor inventory of service items (cutlery, napkins, etc.) and report shortages
	Ensure compliance with health, safety, and hygiene regulations


Requirements


	Minimum of OND / HND or equivalent qualification
	2–4 years of experience in a restaurant or hospitality environment
	Prior supervisory experience is an advantage
	Strong leadership and team management skills
	Excellent communication and problem-solving abilities
	Ability to work under pressure and handle difficult situations
	Good organizational and multitasking skills
	Flexibility to work shifts, weekends, and public holidays


Skills & Competencies:


	Leadership and supervision
	Customer service excellence
	Conflict resolution
	Attention to detail
	Time management
	Team coordination.


Salary
N120,000 - N150,000 / Month.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and cover letter to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.
